title: The Dative Case (Dativ)
description: Learn how to say 'to the man', 'for the woman', or 'with the child'. Welcome to Dative!
explanation:
**HOOK**
Imagine you are passing a gift to a friend. You wouldn't just throw it at them; you hand it TO them. In German, instead of adding an extra word for 'to', the articles themselves morph their shape!

**CONCEPT**
The dativ is just your way of saying who is BENEFITTING from something — like giving a gift TO someone, or helping someone.

**RULE**
Dativ completely rewrites all the articles to act like giant neon 'Receiver' signs! Masculine and Neuter become '-m', Feminine turns masculine with '-r', and Plural gets an extra '-n'.

tables.title: Dativ Changes (The Indirect Object)
tables.note: TIP: Masculine and Neuter finally team up and share 'dem/einem'. Feminine jumps to 'der'. Plural throws an 'n' party on the article AND the noun.

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text:
This one trips up almost everyone at first — and that is completely normal! Beginners always forget that Feminine articles change to 'der' in the Dativ case. It feels incredibly wrong to say 'der Frau', but I promise, Germans are just marking her as the receiver!
Also, prepositions like 'mit' (with) ALWAYS force the next word into Dativ.

examples.english: He is playing with the dogs.

exercises.hint: 'danken' forces Dativ! And Frau is feminine (die).
exercises.explanation: Feminine (die) becomes 'der' in the Dative case!

exercises.question: Was passiert mit maskulinen und neutralen Artikeln im Dativ?
exercises.explanation: Both 'der' and 'das' change to 'dem' in Dative!

exercises.hint: Kind is neuter. The word 'mit' always forces Dativ.
exercises.explanation: Neuter gets the '-em' ending for Dative 'a/an': einem!

exercises.question: Ordne die Dativ-Artikel
exercises.hint: Remember: Masculine/Neuter = m, Feminine = r, Plural = n.
exercises.explanation: Perfect matching of the Dative rules!

exercises.question: ACHTUNG! Was ist die Dativ-Form von 'die Frauen' (the women - Plural)?
exercises.hint: Plurals get an extra 'n' on the article AND the word.
exercises.explanation: Since Plural Dative adds 'den' plus an '-n' to the word (Frauen naturally ends in an n), it is 'den Frauen'.

exercises.hint: Remember how crazy feminine gets in Dativ (-r)!

exercises.question: In welchem Satz gibt es einen neutralen Dativ?
exercises.hint: Look for a neuter word ('das') changing to 'dem'.
exercises.explanation: 'hilft' forces Dative. 'Mädchen' is neuter (das), so das -> dem.

exercises.question: Schreibe (das Kind) im Dativ ('to a child'):
exercises.explanation: das Kind becomes einem Kind in Dative!

exercises.question: Ordne den Satz:
exercises.explanation: Freundin (female friend) is feminine, so we use 'einer'.
